county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Anderson County formed 1846 from Houston. County seat is Palestine. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Palestine holds deed, marriage, and probate records from 1846. Anderson County was an early East Texas farming county with cotton plantation economy. Check TSLAC for Republic of Texas-era land grants. Freedmen's Bureau records at NARA for African American families. Palestine was a railroad hub — check railroad employment records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Anderson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Palestine holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Anderson County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Anderson County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1846. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Anderson County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Anderson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Anderson County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Anderson County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Anderson County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Anderson County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Anderson County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Andrews County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Andrews. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Andrews has records from county organization in 1910 (county created 1876 but not organized until 1910). West Texas ranching and oil county. Permian Basin oil records from 1920s+. Andrews County Heritage Museum. Check Tom Green County for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Andrews County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Andrews holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Andrews County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Andrews County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Andrews
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Andrews County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Andrews County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Angelina County formed 1846 from Nacogdoches. County seat is Lufkin. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lufkin. East Texas lumber industry county — check mill and timber company employment records. Angelina County Historical Commission. Nacogdoches parent county for pre-1846 records. Southern pine lumber operations documented workers extensively. FamilySearch has microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Angelina County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Lufkin holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Aransas County formed 1871 from Refugio. County seat is Rockport. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rockport. Gulf Coast county with fishing and ranching heritage. Aransas County was carved from Refugio — check Refugio for pre-1871 records. Some records damaged in 1919 hurricane. Maritime records at NARA. Texas Maritime Museum in Rockport.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Aransas County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1871; County Clerk in Rockport holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Archer County formed 1858 from Fannin. County seat is Archer City. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Archer City. North Texas frontier county organized 1880. Ranching heritage. Check Fannin County for earlier land records. Archer County Museum. Wichita Falls area genealogical resources serve the region.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Archer County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Archer City holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Archer County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Archer County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Archer County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Archer County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Armstrong County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Claude. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Claude. Panhandle county organized 1890. JA Ranch (Charles Goodnight) covered much of Armstrong County — ranch records at Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum in Canyon. Very sparse early population. Check Bexar County land records for pre-organization.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Armstrong County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Claude holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Atascosa County formed 1856 from Bexar. County seat is Jourdanton. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jourdanton. South Texas brush country — ranching and farming. Mexican land grant heritage; some Spanish colonial records at GLO. Atascosa County was part of Bexar — check San Antonio for pre-1856 records. Strong Mexican-American community records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Atascosa County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Jourdanton holds deed books

county_metadata.parent_county: Municipality of Austin (Old Three Hundred colony)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Austin County formed 1837 from Municipality of Austin (Old Three Hundred colony). County seat is Bellville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bellville. One of Stephen F. Austin's Old Three Hundred colony municipalities. Austin County Historical Society. Some of the oldest Anglo-American settlement records in Texas — Republic of Texas era. Cat Spring Agricultural Society (German settlers, 1856) records. Czech and German immigrant church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Austin County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Bellville holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Austin County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1837. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Austin County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Austin County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Austin County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Austin County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Austin County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Austin County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bailey County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Muleshoe. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Muleshoe. Panhandle county organized 1918. Farming and ranching. Muleshoe Heritage Center. Very sparse pre-organization population — check Bexar County for pre-organization land records. Bailey County Journal newspaper archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bailey County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Muleshoe holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bandera County formed 1856 from Bexar, Uvalde. County seat is Bandera. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bandera. Hill Country county with Polish and German settlers. Frontier Times Museum in Bandera. St. Stanislaus Catholic Church records (Polish community from 1855). Bandera was known as the 'Cowboy Capital of the World.' Cypress mill and shingle-making industry records. Check Bexar and Uvalde for pre-1856 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bandera County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Bandera holds deed books
